allow: Bash(git commit --amend -m 'Cap auto receive-thread count and make NetManager.Start failure-safe' -m '- Auto receive-thread count is now clamped to [1,4] on Linux. Receive scales sub-linearly \(one thread already handles ~600k pkt/s\), so this leaves the remaining cores for the game simulation instead of oversubscribing on networking. *)

Bash(git commit -m 'Add connection handshake security, IPv6 support, and hardening tests' -m '- Handshake: NetworkServer/Client/NetManager gain ConnectionKey \(token\) and ProtocolVersion. The server now only accepts a peer whose ConnectRequest carries a matching version + token; any other packet from an unknown address is dropped, blocking spoofed/garbage/incompatible connects. Defaults \(key=0, version=1\) keep existing behavior. *)
